digest.top_line: ROADS: surg bed recurrence 1% (GammaTile Cs-131 brachy) vs 12% (post-op cavity SRS) in resected brain mets >2cm, a randomized challenge to cavity-SRS SOC.
digest.tldr: Two RT wins anchor the day: ROADS (CNS) makes intraoperative Cs-131 brachy a randomized contender against cavity SRS, and Neo-CRAG (upper-GI) is the positive outlier adding neoadj chemoRT to perioperative XELOX (OS HR 0.78) where TOPGEAR/CRITICS were null. Prostate news runs genomics-led: TALAPRO-3 plus Decipher-guided intensification atop RT+ADT.

sites.intro: Genomic stratification dominates: TALAPRO-3 extends PARP+ARSI to 1L HRR-deficient mHSPC, while Decipher reads (ENZAMET, NRG classifier) refine who gets intensification atop definitive RT+ADT.

studies.name: ARACOG (AFT-47)
studies.tldr: Worst-domain cognitive decline (MCCD) -15.8% (DAR) vs -36.1% (ENZ) at 24wk, p=0.009; darolutamide spared cognition vs enzalutamide.
details: 🔍 Randomized open-label phase II, N=111; advanced PC across mHSPC, nmCRPC, mCRPC, stratified by age (<65 / 65-80 / >80)
🔍 1° EP: % change baseline→24wk in Maximally Changed Cognitive Domain (MCCD), 5 remote CANTAB modules
🔗 Directionally consistent with darolutamide's lower blood-brain-barrier penetration vs enzalutamide
⚠️ Open-label: pts self-perform CANTAB tasks unblinded; expectation/effort bias not excluded
⚠️ MCCD is each arm's maximally-changed domain (derived metric, different domain per arm), not a validated clinical cognition endpoint
⚠️ Small N (~48 vs 47 analyzed), single 24-wk readout; durability of the gap untested
⚠️ Crossover permitted; crossover pts scored at time of crossover within randomized arm

verdict.rationale: Randomized head-to-head hitting prespecified cognitive 1° EP; aligns with darolutamide's recognized lower CNS penetration. Small open-label phase II and surrogate MCCD endpoint keep it short of practice-changing.
verdict.audience: Advanced prostate cancer (mHSPC, nmCRPC, mCRPC) on AR pathway inhibitor

open_questions: Durability of cognitive difference beyond 24 weeks
Whether MCCD changes translate to patient-relevant function or QoL
Consistency of benefit across HSPC and CRPC disease states

studies.name: A-DREAM
studies.tldr: 41% treatment-free with eugonadal testosterone recovery at 18mo (1° EP met, one-sided p=0.0249) after interrupting ADT+ARPI in deep-responding mHSPC.
details: 🔍 Single-arm phase 2 (ALLIANCE A-DREAM); N=78 eligible, enrolled 07/2022-03/2024
🔍 Eligible: mHSPC (64.9% low-volume), PSA<0.2 after 18-24mo ADT + ≥12mo ARPI; interrupt both
📐 1° EP statistically met: 80% CI 33.1-48.9%, one-sided p 0.0249
📊 Time to eugonadal T: median 9.0mo (95% CI 8.4-12.4); 67.9% recovered

subdetails: RT as non-protocol salvage during interruption: 4 (5.1%)

details: ⚠️ 4 of 29 who resumed ADT+ARPI per protocol later progressed (2 rPD, 1 PDu, 1 PET), stopped original ARPI
🔗 Tests full ADT+ARPI interruption in the ARPI era, unlike legacy intermittent-ADT trials; no randomized OS comparator here
⚠️ Single-arm, no continuous-therapy control; durability of 41% off-tx beyond 2yr and survival impact unknown

verdict.rationale: Single-arm phase 2, no continuous-therapy comparator; only selected deep responders; median 26.9mo f/u too short to judge durability or survival impact of interruption.
verdict.audience: mHSPC, deep responders (PSA<0.2) after ≥18mo ADT + ≥12mo ARPI

studies.name: NRG Clinico-Transcriptomic Risk Stratification (Abstract 5000)
studies.tldr: 22-gene genomic classifier reclassifies ~¼ of NCCN ≥HR pts, refining who gets abiraterone intensification atop definitive RT+ADT in localized high-risk prostate.
details: 🔍 Combines NCCN clinical risk with a 22-gene genomic classifier (GC) into one clinico-transcriptomic (CT) risk score
📊 GC independently improves prognostic estimates beyond clinical variables for MFS, DM, and OS (p<0.001)

details.text: 📊 Clinical vs 22-gene biomarker risk concordance (% of NCCN ≥HR pts)

details: 📊 Discordant pts are the decision target: biomarker can upstage a clinically-lower pt or downstage a clinically-higher one
💊 Proposed algorithm: CT HR (≤2 points) → RT+ADT; CT VHR (≥3 points) → RT+ADT + abiraterone
🔗 AAP-benefit magnitude is imported from STAMPEDE M0 (Attard 2022); not prospectively tested by GC subgroup here
🔍 GC bins: <0.6 lower, 0.6-0.85 HR, >0.85 VHR; extends prior VHR work (Spratt JCO 2018)
⚠️ Prognostic ≠ predictive: GC stratifies risk but doesn't prove the high-GC group differentially benefits from added AAP
⚠️ Classifier development is retrospective; GC-guided intensification not yet tested in a randomized trial

verdict.rationale: Prognostic classifier presented as treatment-selection tool; AAP benefit by GC group inferred from STAMPEDE, not a biomarker-treatment interaction. No prospective predictive validation.

open_questions: Prospective validation of GC-guided AAP intensification
Whether biomarker-upstaged pts gain from AAP added to RT+ADT
Cost and access of 22-gene classifier in routine workup

studies.name: ENZAMET + Decipher
studies.tldr: OS HR 0.75 for docetaxel triplet vs doublet in Decipher >0.85 (interaction p=0.04); docetaxel avoidable if Decipher ≤0.85 in mHSPC.
details: 🔍 ENZAMET biomarker substudy; Decipher (DPMC) genomic classifier dichotomized at >0.85; docetaxel-evaluable cohort N=320
📊 Decipher >0.85 marks a poor-prognosis subset on ADT+enza doublet; adding docetaxel appears to neutralize that genomic disadvantage
📊 Clinical read: Decipher >0.85 → consider docetaxel intensification; ≤0.85 → docetaxel likely avoidable
⚠️ Docetaxel use was not randomized in ENZAMET (clinician/patient choice); doce-vs-no-doce compared by propensity-score / IPTW weighting
⚠️ Authors note residual imbalance after propensity scoring; the docetaxel-by-Decipher interaction is exploratory, not confirmatory
⚠️ High-Decipher triplet benefit is directional: the docetaxel HR's 95% CI crosses 1; significance rests on the interaction term, not the subgroup HR
🔗 Consistent with CHAARTED / STAMPEDE: docetaxel OS benefit concentrated in higher-risk disease; here genomics, not just metastatic volume, flags benefiters
📊 Low-volume subgroup: triplet directionally favored in high-Decipher pts, but N small and CI uninformative; no reliable effect size in source

columns: Doce vs no-doce OS HR, unweighted
Doce vs no-doce OS HR, IPTW

verdict.rationale: Post-hoc Decipher subgroup; docetaxel use non-randomized (propensity-weighted, residual imbalance acknowledged); interaction p=0.04 but triplet HR 0.75 CI crosses 1. Hypothesis-generating.

open_questions: Prospective validation of Decipher-guided docetaxel intensification
Does genomic selection extend to ARSI triplets beyond enzalutamide?
Optimal Decipher cutpoint for docetaxel benefit

studies.name: TALAPRO-3
studies.tldr: 3-yr rPFS 77% vs 56% (HR 0.48, 0.36-0.65, p<0.001) adding talazoparib to enza+ADT in HRR-deficient metastatic prostate cancer.
details: 📊 1° EP rPFS, 3-yr landmark (ITT): 77% (67-85) vs 56% (50-62)
📐 ITT events 67/300 (tala) vs 126/299 (placebo); tala median rPFS not reached
📊 Non-BRCA subgroup 3-yr rPFS 76% (69-82) vs 60% (52-67)
⚠️ Benefit deepest in BRCA-altered, attenuated in non-BRCA; HRR-wide result driven by the BRCA subset
🔍 Phase 3 double-blind: talazoparib vs placebo added to enza+ADT; HRR-deficiency required for entry (~300 pts/arm)
🔍 Castration-sensitive setting (enza+ADT backbone, long control rPFS) — extends PARP+ARSI from mCRPC into 1L mHSPC
🔗 vs TALAPRO-2 (1L mCRPC): same tala+enza combo, moved one line earlier
⚠️ rPFS is a surrogate; OS not reported in source, so survival benefit remains unproven
⚠️ Applies only to HRR-deficient pts; requires upfront HRR/BRCA testing to act on

verdict.rationale: Phase 3 double-blind, HRR-selected; primary rPFS hit decisively (HR 0.48), moving PARP+ARSI into 1L mHSPC where it isn't current SOC. OS immature.
verdict.audience: HRR-deficient metastatic prostate cancer, first-line, enza+ADT backbone

open_questions: Overall survival benefit, not yet mature

sites.intro: Bladder-preservation momentum: RAD-IO clears its GO bar for durvalumab added to 55Gy/20fr chemoRT, with EV-based sparing emerging as the comparator.

studies.name: RAD-IO
studies.tldr: 12-mo DFS 80% (40/50, 95% CI 0.67-0.89), clearing the ≥75% GO bar for durvalumab added to 55Gy/20fr chemoRT in MIBC bladder preservation.
details: 📊 1° EP met: 12-mo DFS rate 40/50 (80%), 95% CI 0.67-0.89; cleared pre-set ≥75% GO threshold (NO-GO <60%).
🔍 Single-arm multi-stage feasibility/safety trial, N=55 MIBC; no randomised comparator, benchmarked vs prior CRT data.
💊 Durvalumab neoadjuvant + concurrent + 12mo adjuvant, added to 5FU + mitomycin C chemoRT.
📐 RT: 55Gy/20fr to bladder; node-positive pts add 46Gy/20fr elective nodal (stage 1 expansion, first 6 N+ pts).
⚠️ Safety: AEs reported 'in line with component parts' (chemoRT + durvalumab); no G3+ rates given in source.
⚠️ DFS denominator 50: 2 pending + 3 not evaluable excluded; 'very high bladder preservation' claimed but no numeric rate in source.
🔗 Benchmark BC2001 (JCO 2016): chemo-vs-no-chemo DFS HR 0.78 (0.60-1.02), p=0.07; RAD-IO's historical reference, not a within-trial comparison.

studies.name: PREPEC
studies.tldr: Pre-pectoral implant improved 24mo BREAST-Q chest well-being +4.8 pts (1.0-8.7, p=0.01) vs sub-pectoral, but more unplanned implant loss (21% vs 15%).
details: 🔍 International randomized trial (OPBC-02): pre- vs sub-pectoral implant-based reconstruction after skin- or nipple-sparing mastectomy, therapeutic or risk-reducing setting
🔍 1° endpoint was patient-reported: BREAST-Q physical well-being (chest) at 24mo, 0-100 scale (higher = better)
⚠️ Modest magnitude: 4.8-pt mean difference on a 0-100 PRO scale; lower 95% CI bound 1.0 leaves per-patient clinical relevance uncertain despite p=0.01
⚠️ Safety tradeoff: pre-pectoral had more unplanned implant/expander loss or replacement, which authors call inconsistent with their non-inferiority hypothesis
📐 The adjusted between-arm implant-loss CI crosses zero, so the safety detriment is directional, not statistically conclusive
🔍 Safety reported by actual (as-treated) positioning while the PRO primary used assigned arms, implying some intraoperative crossover between groups
⚠️ Open-label by necessity (positioning can't be blinded); a subjective PRO primary under an unblinded design risks reporting bias
🔗 Prior pre- vs sub-pectoral data is mostly retrospective; this is a rare randomized PRO comparison, but the primary is a PRO, not a hard clinical-outcome endpoint

verdict.rationale: Randomized PRO primary hit, but modest magnitude (lower CI ~1) and a failed safety non-inferiority keep it below practice-changing; supports the field's pre-pectoral shift.

open_questions: Durability of the well-being benefit beyond 24 months

studies.tldr: Surg bed recurrence 1% (GammaTile brachy) vs 12% (post-op SRS) in resected brain mets >2cm; 2-yr OS 62% vs 36%.
details: 🔍 Randomized, resected brain metastasis >2cm, N=230; GammaTile (Cs-131 intraoperative brachytherapy) vs post-op cavity SRS
📊 Surg bed recurrence near-eliminated: 1% GammaTile vs 12% post-op SRS
📊 Toxicity / competing-risk by arm
⚠️ Higher LMD with GammaTile (10% vs 3%) is the key tradeoff against its local-control gain
⚠️ 2-yr OS gap (62% vs 36%) implausibly large for a cavity-local therapy; suspect baseline systemic-burden imbalance or small N
🔗 Post-op cavity SRS is current SOC for resected brain mets (Mahajan Lancet Oncol 2017; N107C); ROADS positions intraoperative brachy as the comparator
🔍 GammaTile = Cs-131 collagen-tile implant placed at resection: immediate bed dosing, single session, no post-op SRS planning delay
⚠️ Open-label by design (implant vs SRS unblindable); abstract-only ASCO 2026, not yet peer-reviewed

verdict.rationale: Randomized vs current SOC (post-op cavity SRS), primary EPs hit; but open-label, an implausibly large OS gap, and higher LMD with brachy keep it below practice-changing.

open_questions: Whether the 2-yr OS benefit is real or confounded by baseline imbalance

studies.name: OptiTROP-Lung05
studies.tldr: mPFS NR vs 5.7mo, HR 0.35, adding TROP2 ADC sac-TMT to pembro in 1L PD-L1+ NSCLC; OS immature (HR 0.55).
details: 💊 Sac-TMT (sacituzumab tirumotecan, SKB264/MK-2870): TROP2-directed ADC, here added to pembrolizumab as a chemo-free 1L doublet
📊 ORR 70.2% vs 42.0% (sac-TMT+pembro vs pembro)
🔍 Phase 3, open-label, multicenter; N=413 randomized 1:1; 1° EP PFS by BICR (blinded central review)
🔍 Eligibility: untreated stage IIIB/IIIC or IV NSCLC, PD-L1 TPS≥1%, EGFR/ALK wild-type, ECOG 0-1; stratified by histology, TPS, ECOG
📊 PFS benefit consistent across both PD-L1 strata, numerically larger in the TPS 1-49% subgroup than ≥50%

details.text: ⚠️ Comparator caveat: control was pembro monotherapy
subdetails: Pembro mono used as control across all enrolled TPS≥1%
For TPS 1-49%, real-world 1L standard is chemo-IO, not pembro mono
So relative benefit in the 1-49% stratum likely overstated vs current SOC

details: ⚠️ Open-label; PFS by BICR mitigates assessment bias, but ORR and investigator-PFS are unblinded
⚠️ OS descriptive/immature at 10.5mo median f/u; HR 0.55 a favorable trend, not the prespecified final OS analysis
🔗 Control mPFS 5.7mo aligns with KEYNOTE-042 1L pembro-mono, validating the comparator for the PD-L1≥50% population

verdict.rationale: Phase 3 primary PFS (BICR) hit decisively vs pembro mono, but open-label, OS immature at 10.5mo f/u, and pembro-mono comparator understates SOC for TPS 1-49%.
verdict.audience: Untreated stage IIIB-IV NSCLC, PD-L1 TPS≥1%, EGFR/ALK wild-type

open_questions: Mature OS confirmation with longer follow-up
Comparison vs chemo-IO, the real-world 1L standard
Added toxicity of the ADC+IO combination

studies.name: Neo-CRAG
studies.tldr: Adding neoadj chemoRT (45Gy/25fx) to perioperative XELOX improved DFS (HR 0.75, mDFS 52.7 vs 24.4mo) and OS (HR 0.78, 67.5 vs 37.6mo) in high-risk LAGC.

details.text: 📊 Efficacy, CRT vs CT (chemoRT added to perioperative XELOX)

details: 📊 Pathologic / locoregional signal (RT-attributable), CRT vs CT — comparison values omitted (cell value "0-2" not verified in source)
🔍 Phase 3 open-label RCT, N=620 (310/arm), 13 Chinese centers, ≥cT3N2 gastric/EGJ adenoca, 36.3% EGJ primary
🔍 RT (CRT arm): 45 Gy/25 fx concurrent after chemo cycle 1, with dose-reduced oxaliplatin/capecitabine
💊 Both arms: perioperative XELOX (3 cycles pre + 3 post) + D2 gastrectomy

details.text: ⚠️ Added toxicity with RT (G3+), CRT vs CT

details: ⚠️ Non-FLOT backbone (XELOX); current perioperative SOC is FLOT, so whether RT adds to a stronger backbone is untested
🔗 vs TOPGEAR and CRITICS, both null for adding RT to perioperative chemo: Neo-CRAG is the positive outlier
⚠️ Median OS gap (67.5 vs 37.6mo) much larger than HR 0.78 implies; check proportional-hazards assumption / tail stability

verdict.rationale: Phase 3 RCT, primary DFS endpoint hit + OS positive, adds RT where TOPGEAR/CRITICS were null; non-FLOT (XELOX) backbone limits transfer to current SOC.
verdict.audience: High-risk locally advanced gastric/EGJ adenoca, ≥cT3N2, D2 resection

open_questions: Does the RT benefit persist on a FLOT backbone vs XELOX?
Generalizability beyond a Chinese D2-gastrectomy population
Which subgroup (EGJ vs gastric, nodal burden) drives the benefit?

studies.tldr: Discussant round-up on curing GU cancers without sacrificing QoL: bladder-preservation chemoRT ± durvalumab, EV-based sparing, ctDNA-guided adjuvant RCC selection.
details: 🔬 Discussant session (Rini, ASCO 2026) framing the curative-intent vs QoL tension across GU; multiple trials, not one new result
💊 Adding durvalumab to mitomycin/5-FU chemoRT didn't compromise RT or chemo delivery (discussant read)
⚠️ Long-term bladder preservation, function, symptom burden, QoL + safety still need longer f/u (discussant)

details.text: 🔍 EV + pembrolizumab bladder-sparing trials in MIBC

details: 🔗 EV309 is the RT-relevant readout: EV+pembro randomized directly against chemoradiotherapy for bladder preservation, with an OS endpoint
🔍 RAMPART (adjuvant IO, RCC): non-clear cell subgroups underpowered, few pts/events, wide CIs, central path review ongoing
⚠️ Adjuvant IO has no proven benefit in non-clear cell RCC (discussant)
🔍 ctDNA vs pathology for adjuvant RCC selection (Choueiri): pathologic features called a crude selection tool
📊 ctDNA-negative 24-mo DFS 79.9% (pembro, n=344) vs 72.9% (placebo, n=332)
📊 ctDNA-positive (n=30/arm) markedly worse: median DFS 10.1 and 6.4 mo, both arms poor
⚠️ Only 30 ctDNA-positive pts/arm; hypothesis-generating, assay not yet a standard selection tool

figures.caption: RT: full 55 Gy/20 fx 54/54 (100%), no extension/delay 47/54 (87%). Durvalumab completed 33/54 (61%).

open_questions: Long-term QoL and safety of durvalumab added to bladder-preservation chemoRT
Whether EV-based bladder sparing matches chemoradiotherapy
ctDNA-guided patient selection for adjuvant therapy in RCC

studies.name: SPIN Score — Celiac Plexus Radiosurgery
studies.tldr: Pain response 32%→89% across the 0-2 score; severe baseline pain and no prior neurotoxic chemo predict who responds to celiac SRS.

details.text: 📊 Pain response by score (post-hoc derivation cohort)
📐 Independent predictors on multivariate logistic regression

details: 🔍 Post-hoc analysis of 90 evaluable pts from the pivotal phase 2 trial (NCT03323489, Lancet Oncol 2024;25:1070-79)
🔍 Pain response = ≥2-pt drop in average BPI-SF pain, baseline to 3 wks (per protocol)

details.text: 📊 Response by baseline pain cutoff (>6 gave optimal discrimination)

details: Best responders have severe baseline pain plus no prior neurotoxic chemo, supporting earlier celiac SRS before chemo-induced neuropathy
🔗 Parent phase 2 overall pain response 53% (95% CI 42-64%); the score separates subgroups well above and below that

subdetails: Post-hoc exploratory score; internal bootstrap validation only, external validation still pending (per authors)
Response measured at 3 wks only, durability of analgesia not assessed
RT dose / fractionation / target volume not reported in source

verdict.rationale: Post-hoc exploratory score from a 90-pt phase 2 subset; internal bootstrap validation only, no external validation; pain endpoint at 3 weeks. Hypothesis-generating.
verdict.audience: Pancreatic cancer with intractable retroperitoneal/celiac pain

open_questions: External validation of the SPIN score before clinical use
Durability of pain response beyond 3 weeks
Optimal timing of celiac SRS relative to neurotoxic chemotherapy

Lay Summary
• In women having surgery to remove the breast for cancer treatment or prevention, the
optimal positioning of an implant (above the chest muscle, or below it) to rebuild the breast is
currently unclear.
• In the international PREPEC trial that was designed together with patient advocates, women
were assigned by chance (i.e., randomized) to receive the implant above or below the
muscle.
• The primary endpoint of the study was long-term patient-reported physical well-being of the
chest.
• Rebuilding the breast with an implant above the chest muscle resulted in better physical
well-being of the chest after two years (difference of 4.8 points on the BREAST-Q scale).
• However, implants placed above the chest muscle were more likely to undergo unexpected
removal or replacement (21% versus 15% of patients).

Perioperative MIBC: An Evolving Standard
Standard of care is defined by cisplatin-eligibility - with immunotherapy-based "sandwich" regimens
reshaping practice.
Cisplatin-ineligible or declining
• KEYNOTE-905: perioperative enfortumab vedotin + pembrolizumab (EVP) is now standard
• 3 cycles EVP → cystectomy → 6 cycles EV + 14 cycles P:
• NEW: VOLGA: preoperative EV + perioperative durvalumab + tremelimumab
• EV + durva improved EFS & OS vs cystectomy * approved adjuvant therapy; EV + durva/treme improved EFS (press release)?
Cisplatin-eligible
• NIAGARA: pre-op Gem/Cis + perioperative durvalumab (24-mo OS 82.2% vs 75.2% with Gem/Cis alone)
• VESPER: established ddMVAC > Gem/Cis on survival (5-yr OS 66% vs 57%) but greater toxicity*
• NEW: KEYNOTE-B15: perioperative EVP improved OS vs Gem/Cis (under FDA review)

For the sandwich regimens, standard practice continues planned
adjuvant therapy regardless of pathologic response.
Standard practice today

Perioperative EVP: Resume EVP post-cystectomy
Notably, not all patients in perioperative trials completed adjuvant therapy, sometimes due to toxicity
Cisplatin-based chemo alone: Surveillance after pCR is standard

Conclusions
• This single-arm study of amivantamab + lazertinib (n=49) in 1L atypical EGFR-mutated advanced NSCLC
demonstrates a clinically meaningful median OS of ~3.5 years
• Responses were durable regardless of demographics, baseline tumor mutations, and disease characteristic:

with no new safety signals

in both common and atypical EGFR-mutated advanced NSCLC

image_ocr_texts:
The SPIN Score, a simple clinical score to predict pain response following celiac plexus
Abstract # 546246
Board # 194
radiosurgery for retroperitoneal cancer pain in pancreatic cancer.
Yaacov Lawrence Lawrence, Marcin Miszczyk, Dayssy Alexandra Diaz Pardo, Artur Aguiar, Dror Limon, M. Raphael Pfeffer, Michael Buckstein, Aisling S Barry, Adam P. Dicker, Camilla Zimmermann,
Jordan Kharofa, Ofer Margalit, Orly Yariv, Ofir Morag, David Hausner, Maoz Ben-Ayun, Talia Golan, Laura A. Dawson, Zvi Symon
SHEBA
Tel HaShomer
The Benjamin (Beni) Davidai
Department of
Radiation Oncology
Background
Celiac plexus radiosurgery is a novel non-invasive palliative treatment that was
recently added to NCCN guidelines. In the pivotal phase 2 trial (NCT03323489;
Lancet Oncol 2024;25:1070-79), pain response was 53% (95% Cl 42-64%).
Prespecified exploratory analysis identified age and BMI as predictors; however, no
clinically actionable patient selection tool was developed. We sought to identify
additional predictors and create a practical risk stratification score.
Methods
Post-hoc analysis of 90 evaluable patients from the phase 2 trial. Pain response was
defined per protocol (22-point reduction in average pain from baseline to three
weeks, on Brief Pain Inventory-Short Form). Candidate baseline predictors were
examined by univariate and multivariate logistic regression. Only variables
remaining significant in multivariate analysis were included in the final score to
ensure independence and avoid overfitting. Internal validation used bootstrap
resampling (500 iterations with replacement) to calculate optimism-corrected AUC.
Analysis performed using Stata IC/16.1.
Results
Univariate analysis identified 4 significant predictors: neurotoxic chemotherapy
exposure (OR 5.33, 95% Cl 2.13-13.4, p<0.001), baseline pain intensity (OR 1.73,
p=0.003), age (OR 1.06, p=0.014), and therapy line (OR 0.65, p=0.04). On
multivariate analysis, only neurotoxic exposure (OR 5.1, p=0.009) and baseline pain
(OR 1.8, p=0.003) remained independently significant predictors. Age and therapy
line lost significance due to collinearity.
Dose-response analysis
confirmed
pain threshold
optimization: >5
(61.5%
response), >6 (65.8%), >7 (83.3%), >8 (85.7%), with >6 providing optimal
discrimination.
We created the SPIN (Severe Pain + Intact Nerves) score (0-2
points): baseline Pain >6 (+1), No prior Neurotoxic chemotherapy (+1). Response
rates by score: 0 points: 32% (n=31); 1 point: 53% (n=40); 2 points: 89% (n=19). The
2-variable model achieved an apparent AUC=0.716. Following bootstrapping, the
optimism-corrected AUC was= 0.714
Conclusions
• The simple score allows identification of distinct patient subgroups with markedly different probabilities of pain response
following celiac plexus radiosurgery.
• This suggests the intervention should be considered earlier in the disease course, before exposure to neurotoxic agents.
External validation is needed prior to clinical implementation.
